chief_complaint: A 41-year-old male patient presented with atypical abdominal pain followed by massive lower gastrointestinal bleeding.
history: The patient had a 4-year history of intestinal Behcet's disease (BD). He initially presented with symptoms consistent with a recurrence of intestinal BD, including esophageal and anastomotic ulcers. Despite conservative medical treatment, the disease progressed to persistent bleeding.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Computed tomography angiography revealed extravasation around a branch artery of the superior mesenteric artery.
correct_diagnosis: Rupture of the superior mesenteric artery branch in Behcet's disease
differentials: Rupture of the superior mesenteric artery branch in Behcet's disease
Intestinal Behcet's disease with gastrointestinal bleeding
Gastrointestinal bleeding due to aortic dissection
Gastrointestinal bleeding due to aortoenteric fistula
correct_ranking: Rupture of the superior mesenteric artery branch in Behcet's disease
Intestinal Behcet's disease with gastrointestinal bleeding
Gastrointestinal bleeding due to aortic dissection
Gastrointestinal bleeding due to aortoenteric fistula
